sections.paragraph:
Χανάαν, ἡ indecl. (also Χαναάν; כְּנַעַן; Gen 11:31 al.; Philo, Test12Patr, JosAs, Philo, Just.—In Joseph. Χαναναία, ας [Ant. 1, 186]) Canaan, in our lit. (i.e. Ac) the land west of the Jordan, where the patriarchs lived Ac 7:11 (w. Egypt), which God gave to the Hebrews upon their escape fr. Egypt 13:19 (γῆ X.).—FStähelin, Der Name Kanaan: JWackernagel Festschr. 1923, 150–53; BHHW II 926; TLL, Suppl. 1, 371f.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

  1. fr. = from
  2. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

TLL TLL = Thesaurus Linguae Latinae—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1077
